title: 10 best herbal creams for queasy stomach

remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Ginger's digestive stimulant property helps to increase saliva production, which breaks down food and reduces nausea and vomiting by promoting digestion.

Anti-Inflammatory Compound: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compound, gingerol, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can help alleviate symptoms of indigestion, bloating, and queasiness.

Gastrokinetic Agent: The bioactive compounds in herbal ginger creams, such as gingerols and shogaols, work as gastrokinetic agents, helping to stimulate muscle contractions in the stomach that move food through the digestive system more efficiently, reducing nausea and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with queasy stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe digestive issues. The active compound gingerol, found in ginger, has been shown to reduce nausea and vomiting by relaxing the stomach muscles and improving digestion. Additionally, ginger has been used for centuries to alleviate morning sickness and motion sickness, making it a popular remedy for queasy stomachs. Its calming effects can also help reduce stomach discomfort and promote a sense of well-being.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate nausea and reduce stomach discomfort by inhibiting the release of stomach acid and soothing the digestive tract.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols are also known for their anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties, which help to calm the digestive system and prevent vomiting.

Terpenes (specifically, beta-sesquiphellandrene): Beta-sesquiphellandrene, a terpene present in Zingiber officinale, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-nausea effects by reducing inflammation in the stomach and promoting smooth muscle relaxation.
remedy_parts: Stems: Some ginger creams may include stem extracts, which are valued for their ability to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, helping to alleviate queasy stomach symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 tablespoon of dried zingiber officinale root in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Steep the mixture for 10 minutes then strain it through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl.
Combine 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add the infused zingiber officinale liquid to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and refrigerate it for 30 minutes to set.

remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's cooling effect helps to calm digestive discomfort, reducing nausea and stomach spasms by blocking pain receptors in the gut.
Eugenol: Eugenol's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can contribute to queasiness and discomfort.
Volatile Oils: The volatile oils present in peppermint cream, including menthone and limonene, have natural anti-emetic properties that help to alleviate nausea and vomiting.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with queasy stomach because it contains menthol and menthone, natural compounds that ease digestive discomfort. The menthol in peppermint creams has a soothing effect on the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and calming nausea. As a result, peppermint creams can provide fast relief from queasy stomach and help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, motion sickness, and other digestive issues. This natural remedy promotes a sense of calmness and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps with queasy stomach by numbing the stomach lining, reducing inflammation, and alleviating nausea through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.

Royleanone: Royleanone helps with queasy stomach by inhibiting the contraction of smooth muscle in the stomach, reducing spasms, and relaxing the digestive system, thus providing relief from nausea and vomiting.

Piperitenone: Piperitenone helps with queasy stomach by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the stomach, thereby alleviating nausea and other symptoms associated with queasy stomach.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Essential oils extracted from peppermint leaves are commonly used in creams for queasy stomach due to their digestive properties and ability to relax the muscles.
Rhyzomes: Peppermint rhyzomes are used to create a calming and soothing effect, which helps to alleviate stomach discomfort and nausea.
Leaves: The menthol content in peppermint leaves helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach, making them a popular ingredient in creams for queasy stomach.
remedy_recipe: Combine 20 grams of dried mentha x piperita leaves with 400 milliliters of jojoba oil in a heat-resistant glass container.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 30 minutes to infuse the oil with herbal properties.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove the plant material.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax with 50 grams of shea butter in a double boiler to melt the mixture slowly.
Combine the herbal oil with the melted wax and butter mixture in a 1:1 ratio to create a smooth cream consistency.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lavender's antispasmodic compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to nausea and queasiness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can exacerbate symptoms of a queasy stomach such as bloating, gas, and discomfort.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) properties: The calming effects of lavender can help to alleviate anxiety and stress, common triggers for nausea and queasiness, by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with queasy stomach because of its soothing and calming properties. The calming effects of lavender can help to reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for stomach upset. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help to alleviate digestive discomfort and ease nausea. The antispasmodic properties of lavender can also help to relax the digestive muscles, reducing queasiness and promoting a sense of calm. This natural remedy can provide relief from queasy stomach.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene compound that helps to alleviate nausea and vomiting by reducing inflammation and modulating the gut-brain axis.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene ester that has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress-related queasiness.
Apigenin: A phenolic compound with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which helps to relax the muscles and calm the digestive system to alleviate queasy stomach.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to ease stomach discomfort.
Flowers: They are used for their antispasmodic and antacid properties, which can help to alleviate nausea and heartburn.
Stems: They are used due to their ability to aid digestion and reduce inflammation in the stomach, thereby alleviating queasiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning when the dew is still present on the petals.
Dry the freshly harvested flowers in a warm place for 7 to 10 days at room temperature.
Combine 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut oil or sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the dried flowers in the carrier oil for 2 to 3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and use as a base for cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like apigenin and luteolin, which help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and nausea.
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic effects of chamomile can help to relax muscles in the stomach, reducing cramps and spasms that can contribute to queasy feelings.

Calming and soothing flavonoids: Chamomile creams contain flavonoids like apigenin and luteolin, which have a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to reduce anxiety and stress-related digestive issues.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with queasy stomach because of its soothing and calming properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help to relax the digestive tract and reduce muscle spasms that may be contributing to the queasiness. Additionally, chamomile has a natural anti-nausea effect, which can help to alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting associated with queasy stomach.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: As a flavonoid, apigenin has a calming effect on the stomach and can help reduce inflammation, which in turn can alleviate queasy stomach symptoms.
Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help soothe stomach cramps and reduce nausea associated with queasy stomach.

Chamazulene: As a sesquiterpene, chamazulene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining from damage, thereby alleviating queasy stomach symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe digestive issues and reduce nausea.
Leaves: They are used for their sedative and antispasmodic effects, which help relax the muscles in the stomach and alleviate queasiness.
Seeds: They are used due to their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help release gas in the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 part dried flowers in 3 parts carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bowl.
Combine the infused oil with 1 part beeswax and 1 part shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture until the beeswax and shea butter are fully melted and well combined.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and thicken for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Fennel cream can help relieve muscle spasms in the digestive tract, reducing cramping and discomfort associated with queasy stomachs.
Carminative properties: Fennel's carminative effects help to expel gas from the digestive system, reducing bloating and discomfort caused by digestive issues like indigestion and nausea.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel cream may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to queasy stomach symptoms such as stomach pain, bloating, and cramping.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, creams helps with queasy stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe digestive issues. The essential oils present in fennel, such as anethole and fenchone, have been shown to calm the stomach and reduce nausea. Additionally, fennel has a gentle, carminative effect, helping to alleviate gas and bloating. By applying fennel creams topically, individuals can experience relief from queasy stomach and related discomforts.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Anethole is a terpene that acts as a carminative, helping to relieve gas and reduce stomach discomfort associated with queasiness.
Apiol: Apiol, a phenolic compound, has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties and may help alleviate nausea and stomach cramps caused by digestive issues.
Foeniculin: Foeniculin, a sesquiterpene, is believed to have a calming effect on the digestive system, which may help soothe an upset stomach and relieve queasiness.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are commonly used due to their carminative properties, which help to ease digestion and reduce nausea.
Leaves: Fennel leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing an upset stomach and alleviating queasiness.
Roots: Fennel roots contain compounds that help to calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation, making them a popular choice for creams aimed at relieving queasy stomachs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh foeniculum vulgare roots and clean them thoroughly with water for 30 minutes.
Steam 300g of the cleaned roots for 60 minutes to release their volatile oils.
Mix the steamed roots with 500g of sweet almond oil and 200g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to dissolve the beeswax completely.
Strain the cooled mixture through a cheesecloth and bottle the resulting cream for later use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxerol, can soothe and calm digestive inflammation, reducing nausea and discomfort associated with an upset stomach.

Digestive stimulant: The inulin and fructooligosaccharides present in dandelion cream can stimulate digestion, improve gut motility, and reduce symptoms of indigestion and bloating often experienced during queasiness.
Antioxidant: Dandelion's rich antioxidant content can help neutralize free radicals that may contribute to nausea and stomach upset, promoting overall digestive health and reducing oxidative stress.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with queasy stomach because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe digestive issues. The cream contains inulin, a prebiotic that promotes a healthy gut microbiome, reducing inflammation and calming digestive discomfort. Additionally, the cream's astringent properties help to reduce bloating and alleviate nausea, providing quick relief from queasy stomach symptoms. The natural compounds in Taraxacum officinale cream work together to promote a balanced digestive system, providing a natural solution for stomach issues.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: A type of fructan, inulin helps alleviate queasy stomach by promoting gut health and regulating bowel movements, which can ease digestive discomfort.
Luteolin: A flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, luteolin helps calm digestive issues by reducing inflammation in the stomach lining and protecting against oxidative stress.

Taraxasterol: A triterpene saponin, taraxasterol has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe queasy stomach by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and promoting a balanced gut microbiome.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their high content of inulin, a natural anti-inflammatory that helps soothe the stomach.
Roots: Roots are used for their bitter compounds, which have been traditionally used to aid digestion and alleviate nausea.
Seeds: Seeds are used due to their inulin content as well as their potential to help regulate bowel movements and alleviate queasy stomach symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Infuse taraxacum officinale flowers and leaves in 2 cups of carrier oil for 2 to 3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Combine 1/4 cup of the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil.
Melt the beeswax mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 10 to 15 minutes.
Pour the melted mixture into a clean container and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to possess antioxidant properties, which can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in Ginkgo biloba have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) such as bloating, cramping, and diarrhea.

Gastroprotective properties: Ginkgo biloba has been found to possess gastroprotective effects by reducing the production of acid in the stomach and increasing mucus production, which can help soothe and protect the stomach lining.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with queasy stomach because of its unique ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. The antioxidants and flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba creams have been shown to soothe digestive issues, alleviate nausea, and calm stomach discomfort. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, Ginkgo biloba creams can also help alleviate the emotional factors that contribute to queasy stomach, providing a natural and effective solution to alleviate digestive discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation and alleviate queasy stomach symptoms.

Terpenoids (specifically, bilobalide): Bilobalide, a terpenoid found in Ginkgo biloba, may help protect the stomach lining, reduce inflammation, and improve blood flow to the stomach, thereby alleviating queasy stomach symptoms.

Ginkgolides: These triterpene lactones have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce nausea and alleviate queasy stomach symptoms by inhibiting the release of inflammatory mediators and improving blood flow to the stomach.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate stomach discomfort.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba have been used to treat digestive issues, including queasy stomachs, due to their ability to soothe the stomach lining.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea and stomach discomfort, due to their ability to relax the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Extract 40 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and 20 grams of ginkgo biloba seeds from 500 grams of raw material.
Combine the extracted ginkgo biloba leaves and seeds with 1000 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a 1:10 ratio.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours and then filter it through a 0.2 micrometer filter to obtain the extract.
Mix 500 grams of petroleum jelly with 10 grams of the ginkgo biloba extract and 5 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes to achieve a smooth and even consistency.

remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric contains curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of nausea and bloating.
Antioxidants: Herbal turmeric creams contain antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to stomach upset and queasiness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with queasy stomach because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, curcumin, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate nausea and discomfort. Additionally, curcuma longa creams may help stimulate digestion and improve nutrient absorption, further easing queasy stomach symptoms. By soothing the digestive system, curcuma longa creams can provide relief from queasy stomach and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: A polyphenolic compound that acts as a natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, reducing inflammation and soothing the stomach lining to alleviate queasiness.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): A terpenoid compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory and anti-emetic properties, helping to calm the stomach and reduce nausea and vomiting.
Turmerone: A sesquiterpene compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate queasiness in the stomach.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part for making creams due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and digestive benefits.
Roots: Roots of Curcuma longa are also widely used in creams for their ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce nausea due to their bioactive compounds.
Stems: Some studies suggest that stems of Curcuma longa may have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate queasy stomach symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes by selecting firm and unblemished ones.
Grind the harvested rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered rhizomes with 10g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler at 150f for 30 minutes.
Add 10g of shea butter and 5g of vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the double boiler and let it cool at room temperature for 1 hour before applying it.

remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Valerian cream's sedative properties can help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms, which can alleviate queasiness.

Gastroprotective properties: Valerian has been shown to have gastroprotective effects, which means it can help protect the stomach lining from irritation and inflammation, reducing symptoms of queasy stomach and digestive discomfort.

The active compounds in valerian root, such as valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, have a relaxing effect on the stomach muscles and intestines, reducing spasms and inflammation. This helps to alleviate nausea, vomiting, and digestive discomfort associated with queasy stomach, making it a popular natural remedy for individuals experiencing motion sickness, morning sickness, and other stomach-related issues.
remedy_constituents: Isovaleramate: Isovaleramate is a sesquiterpene derivative that acts as a GABA receptor agonist, helping to reduce anxiety and calm the stomach, alleviating queasiness.
Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid is a sesquiterpene that has sedative and anxiolytic properties, reducing stress and anxiety, which can contribute to queasy stomach.

remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian root is commonly used in creams for queasy stomach due to its sedative properties and ability to ease digestive issues.
Leaves: Valerian leaves are used in creams for their calming effects and ability to soothe digestive problems.
Buds: Valerian buds are used in creams for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate queasy stomach symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 30 grams of dried valeriana officinalis root in 500 milliliters of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discard the solids.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of candelilla wax in a double boiler.
Melt the wax mixture and add 10 milliliters of the infused oil to create a uniform consistency.
Pour the melted mixture into small glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation that may be contributing to nausea and queasiness.

Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antacid and anti-ulcer properties, which can help neutralize stomach acid and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach from irritation, reducing symptoms of indigestion and nausea.

Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract, which can contribute to queasiness and discomfort after eating or due to other factors.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with queasy stomach because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The root of the licorice plant contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which soothes the digestive tract and reduces inflammation. This, in turn, can help alleviate symptoms of queasiness and stomach upset. remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin helps with queasy stomach by inhibiting the production of gastric acid and reducing inflammation in the stomach lining, thus providing relief from nausea and heartburn.

Licopyranocoumarin: This phenolic compound has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the stomach, thereby alleviating symptoms of a queasy stomach.

Flavonoids (mainly isoliquiritin and liquiritin): These plant-based compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to protect the stomach lining from damage, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of nausea and heartburn associated with a queasy stomach.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin compound, is extracted from the roots to help soothe an upset stomach due to its anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Licorice root extract, derived from the roots, contains compounds that can help alleviate nausea and queasiness.
Roots: The roots are also a source of flavonoids, which may aid in reducing inflammation and providing relief from stomach discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Extract 50g of glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 500ml of distilled water at 70-80°c for 30 minutes.
Filter the mixture and add 20g of beeswax and 10g of candelilla wax to it.
Heat the mixture at 60-70°c until the waxes melt and emulsify with the herbal extract.
Add 50g of coconut oil and 20g of shea butter to the mixture and heat it for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with queasy stomach because they contain the natural herb sage, which has been used for centuries to alleviate digestive issues. The essential oils in sage have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea and discomfort. When applied topically, these oils can ease stomach cramps and spasms, calming the digestive system and promoting a sense of relief from queasiness.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Herbal sage creams contain compounds like rosmarinic acid, which help to relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract and reduce spasms that can cause nausea and vomiting.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining and alleviate symptoms of queasiness and indigestion.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzome of the sage plant contains sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and calm digestive discomfort associated with queasy stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried salvia officinalis leaves and mix with 10ml of olive oil in a small bowl.
Combine the herb mixture with 100ml of warm water and let it steep for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discard solids and set aside to cool.
Add 20g of beeswax pellets and 10ml of coconut oil to the cooled liquid, heat in a double boiler until melted.
Remove from heat, whip with a hand blender for 2 minutes until thickened and creamy, pour into sterilized jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with queasy stomach because they possess natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that soothe digestive issues. The herbal extracts in thyme creams work to calm the stomach, reducing nausea and vomiting associated with queasiness. Additionally, thyme's carvacrol content has been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the gut, promoting a healthy balance of gut flora. By applying topically, thyme creams can help alleviate stomach discomfort and promote overall digestive wellness.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Thyme's antispasmodic property helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing cramps and spasms that can exacerbate nausea.
Anti-inflammatory property: Thyme's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive system, which can contribute to queasy stomach symptoms such as bloating and discomfort.
Digestive stimulant property: Thyme's digestive stimulant property helps to improve digestion and increase appetite, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with queasiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to soothe and calm an upset stomach.
Seeds: Thyme seeds contain volatile oils that have natural carminative properties, helping to reduce bloating and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves from your garden or local market on a sunny morning.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind 1 cup of dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder and sift through a mesh screen.
Mix 10 tablespoons of powdered thymus vulgaris with 20 tablespoons of cooled coconut oil in a double boiler, heating slowly over medium heat for 30 minutes.
Pour the infused oil mixture into sterilized glass jars, sealing tightly and allowing to cool at room temperature for at least 2 hours before storing in a dark cupboard.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats creams helps with queasy stomach because of its soothing and calming properties. The creamy texture coats the stomach lining, providing a barrier against irritants that can trigger queasiness. Herbal ingredients in oats creams, such as peppermint and ginger, further help to ease digestion and alleviate nausea. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Oats contain compounds like avenanthramides, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach.
Soil-binding properties: Oats are able to bind to toxins and other substances in the gut, helping to remove them from the body and reducing irritation and discomfort associated with queasiness.

Soothing and emollient properties: Oat cream's rich, moisturizing ingredients can help soothe and protect the lining of the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort, and providing a calming effect on an upset stomach.

remedy_recipe: Measure 2 tablespoons of dried avena sativa powder into a clean glass bowl.
Combine measured avena sativa powder with 8 ounces of distilled water in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches 180°f.
Strain the hot liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass bowl.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil with the cooled herbal infusion and whip until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop creams helps with queasy stomach because of its unique blend of soothing herbs. The cream's active ingredients, such as passionflower and chamomile, work together to calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms of nausea and discomfort. The gentle, non-irritating properties of maypop also help to ease inflammation and cramping in the stomach, providing quick relief from queasiness. remedy_properties: Passionflower extract: Reduces anxiety and nervousness, which are common causes of queasiness, by binding to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and calming the digestive system.
Chamomile essential oil: Soothes the stomach lining and reduces inflammation, helping to alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting, while also promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.

Ginger extract: Relieves digestive discomfort by reducing inflammation and spasms in the stomach, intestines, and esophagus, making it an effective natural remedy for queasy stomachs, nausea, and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, may help soothe digestive issues and reduce nausea.
Fruits: Contain natural antacids that can help neutralize stomach acid and alleviate symptoms of upset stomach.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves according to traditional methods ensuring quality and quantity for processing.
Dry the harvested herbs in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for four hours or until crispy and brittle textures achieved.
Combine one part dried passiflora incarnata with two parts beeswax and six parts coconut oil in a double boiler for gentle heat processing.
Heat the mixture under constant stirring for approximately thirty minutes or until the waxes are fully melted and incorporated into the herbal infusion.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify at room temperature before whipping and transferring to an air-tight container for storage and usage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with queasy stomach because its calming properties soothe digestive issues, alleviating symptoms of nausea and discomfort. intro_desc: Herbal creams for queasy stomach are topical remedies that help alleviate nausea and vomiting by applying a soothing balm to the skin. They work by releasing the active herbal ingredients, which are then absorbed through the skin, providing quick relief from queasy stomach symptoms. The best herbal creams for queasy stomach include Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Foeniculum vulgare, Ginkgo biloba, Mentha x piperita, Aloe barbadensis, Cinchona officinalis, Cinnamomum verum, Zanthoxylum bungeanum, and Angelica archangelica. If left untreated, queasy stomach can lead to dehydration, malnutrition, and disrupted daily routines, impacting work, social life, and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to queasy stomach that are treated with herbal creams are digestive issues such as indigestion, bloating, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Herbal creams containing ingredients like peppermint, chamomile, and ginger can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce symptoms of nausea, cramps, and diarrhea. These creams can also be used to treat conditions like inflammatory bowel disease and gastritis.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with queasy stomach is a blend of Ginger, Peppermint, and Chamomile. Ginger cream soothes digestive issues and reduces nausea, while Peppermint cream eases cramps and calms the stomach. Chamomile cream promotes relaxation and reduces inflammation. Together, these creams create a natural trifecta that tackles queasiness from multiple angles. Apply a small amount of each cream to the abdomen and massage gently for 5-10 minutes, 2-3 times a day, to experience their soothing effects and find relief from a queasy stomach.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with queasy stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory and digestive properties. The active compound, gingerol, found in ginger, has been shown to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness, morning sickness, and other gastrointestinal issues. Ginger's ability to stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility also helps alleviate queasiness. As a result, many people have found relief from queasy stomach with the use of Zingiber officinale creams, making it a popular natural remedy for digestive discomfort.

Gingerol-6 and Gingerol-8 (Zingerone): These components have antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the stomach, which can contribute to queasiness and nausea.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh zingiber officinale roots with a clean and sanitized knife.
Wash the roots in cold running water to remove dirt and debris for 5 minutes.
Grate 4 ounces of the washed zingiber officinale roots using a food processor for 30 seconds.
Infuse the grated zingiber officinale roots in 8 ounces of coconut oil in a double boiler at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Strain the infused coconut oil through a cheesecloth into a glass container and discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Extract 30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in a mixture of 500 milliliters of ethanol and 500 milliliters of water.
Steep the mixture for 2 hours at a temperature of 60 degrees celsius then filter it through a cheesecloth.
Combine 100 grams of glycerin with the extracted mixture and stir it well for 10 minutes.
Heat the mixture to 70 degrees celsius and hold the temperature for 30 minutes then let it cool.
Mix 200 milliliters of distilled water with the cooled mixture and stir it well to form a smooth cream.

remedy_recipe: Melt 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes stirring occasionally.

Add the water and aloe mixture to the melted beeswax mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.
